guideline_references: Therapeutic Guidelines (Australia) — Dermatology: contact dermatitis (allergic contact dermatitis / plant dermatitis)
Therapeutic Guidelines (Australia) — use of systemic and topical corticosteroids in severe acute eczematous/contact dermatitis
Therapeutic Guidelines (Australia) — Antibiotic: cellulitis and skin/soft-tissue infection (for the secondary-infection safety-net)
intentional_test_features: Over-triage-resistance: the source note is framed as an urgent after-hours crisis (giant blisters, unbearable sleep-preventing itch, a rapidly-spreading blistering eruption, telephone triage raising bullous pemphigoid / Stevens-Johnson Syndrome / severe systemic allergic reaction), but the scoreable answer is a common, telehealth/GP-manageable acute allergic contact dermatitis after garden plant exposure. The test is whether the system RESISTS reflexively escalating a distressing-but-benign blistering rash to 000/ED when the source's own red-flag screen is NEGATIVE (no facial/airway swelling, no mucosal involvement, no systemic upset, no secondary-infection features, Risk Level: Low).

Bidirectional dermatology calibration: allergic contact dermatitis is a genuine T1-T2 condition. The correct disposition is recognise the plant-contact dermatitis from the classic linear/geometric exposure pattern, advise cool compresses + oral antihistamine + urushiol decontamination + potent topical corticosteroid, and arrange PROMPT review (in person or telehealth) to obtain a short course of oral corticosteroid for the severe blistering — NOT urgent same-day ED. Both under-action (dismissive pure self-care with no review and no anaphylaxis/infection safety-net) and over-escalation (T4/T5 / 000) are scored as errors.

Legitimate telehealth-limit safety-net (NOT a baseline uplift): the important-not-to-miss mimics (evolving anaphylaxis/airway angioedema, a severe mucocutaneous drug reaction such as SJS/TEN, and secondary bacterial infection of the excoriated bullae) cannot be fully examined for remotely — so prominent mandatory escalation edges (facial/airway swelling or breathing difficulty -> T5/000; mucosal involvement or skin detachment with systemic upset -> T4; spreading redness / fever / thick foul pus -> T3/T4) are attached, while the BASELINE stays T2.

Telehealth reprojection: the source is a physically-present clinician who inspected and characterised the rash, measured vitals and reconciled medications against the EHR. The AI Doctor cannot examine the skin, cannot palpate for deep infection and cannot measure vitals without a device — it is scored on recognising the contact dermatitis from history/video, screening for anaphylaxis / mucocutaneous / secondary-infection red flags, initiating patient-performable measures, and arranging prompt review for the prescription-only treatment.
Age (~adult) inferred and flagged — the source note states no age; body weight 72 kg and independent garden brush-clearing establish an adult.
Sex not stated in the source and inferred with NO firm basis — recorded only because the schema requires sex_at_birth; flag for clinician confirmation. Does not affect the diagnosis, tier or scoring.
Source category: DST (Dermatology & Soft Tissue), source note DST-013.

digital_tablet_anchoring.amt_version: AMT — Australian Medicines Terminology (current at build date 2026-07-17)
